qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Open redirect via Host header injection in ForceHttps middleware
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns ForceHttps redirect construction from the Host header, while the advisory concerns unbounded request-body collection in Salvo form parsing via Request::form_data(), FormData::read(), multipart handling, and Extractible body sources.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described root cause. The advisory is a denial of service caused by Salvo form parsing reading URL-encoded or multipart request bodies into memory without size limits before validation. The only qualifying finding reports an unrelated Host-header open redirect in the ForceHttps middleware.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-pp9r-xg4c-8j4x describes unbounded memory allocation in form_data(), multipart form parsing, and Extractible body-source parsing, with OOM denial of service as the impact.
force_https_host_header_open_redirect/vulnerability_report.json describes crates/extra/src/force_https.rs building a 308 redirect Location from the client-controlled Host header.

The qualifying finding does not reference the FormData::read(), Request::form_data(), multipart parser, Extractible macro body extraction path, payload size enforcement, or OOM/resource-exhaustion behavior described by the advisory.
